createLetterValuesFunction.name: Create the letterValues function
createLetterValuesFunction.description: Write a letterValues function that returns a dictionary mapping each uppercase letter to its Scrabble point value. Start with the provided list of letter groups and their values, and convert it into a dictionary.

singleLetters.name: Score single letters
singleLetters.description: Write a scrabbleScore function that takes a word and returns its total Scrabble score. Start by getting it working for single letters.

words.name: Score words
words.description: Now get scrabbleScore working with full words of different lengths.

edgeCases.name: Handle edge cases
edgeCases.description: Finally, make sure your function handles edge cases like empty strings and the full alphabet.

letterValues.name: The letterValues function
letterValues.description: The letterValues function should return a complete dictionary of letter scores.

scrabbleLowercaseLetter.description: Calculate the score for a single lowercase letter.

scrabbleUppercaseLetter.description: Calculate the score for a single uppercase letter.

scrabbleValuableLetter.description: Calculate the score for a valuable letter.

scrabbleShortWord.name: Short word
scrabbleShortWord.description: Calculate the score for a short word.

scrabbleShortValuableWord.description: Calculate the score for a short word with valuable letters.

scrabbleMediumWord.name: Medium word
scrabbleMediumWord.description: Calculate the score for a medium-length word.

scrabbleMediumValuableWord.name: Medium, valuable word
scrabbleMediumValuableWord.description: Calculate the score for a medium-length word with valuable letters.

scrabbleLongMixedCaseWord.name: Long, mixed-case word
scrabbleLongMixedCaseWord.description: Calculate the score for a long word with mixed case letters.

scrabbleEnglishLikeWord.name: English-like word
scrabbleEnglishLikeWord.description: Calculate the score for an English-like word.

scrabbleEmptyInput.name: Empty input
scrabbleEmptyInput.description: Calculate the score for an empty string.

scrabbleEntireAlphabet.name: Entire alphabet
scrabbleEntireAlphabet.description: Calculate the score for a string with every letter of the alphabet.

startingPoint.question: Where should I start with the letter values?
startingPoint.answer: Don't write a 26-line lookup by hand. Start with a list of groups, for example `["AEIOULNRST", "DG", "BCMP", "FHVWY", "K", "JX", "QZ"]` and their matching values `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 10]`.

buildLookup.question: How do I turn those groups into a usable lookup?
buildLookup.answer: Build a dictionary by iterating through each group's letters and mapping each individual letter to the group's value. Now `dict["A"]` directly gives you `1`.

lowercaseInput.question: Why don't my lookups work for lowercase input?
lowercaseInput.answer: If your dictionary keys are uppercase, looking up `'a'` won't match `'A'`. Convert each letter to uppercase before looking up its value.

addUpScore.question: How do I add up the score?
addUpScore.answer: Loop through the word, look up each letter's value in your dictionary, and add it to a running total. Return the total at the end.

toUpperCase.description: Convert a string to uppercase (provided by level stdlib)
